Name: Kurukshetra Foot-Soldiers
Anecdote: 'Eighteen armies stood on the field of dharma. The conches blared, and the earth trembled between the two hosts.'

Name: Draupadi
Effect: Draupadi cannot be targeted by enemy card effects.
Anecdote: 'They pulled at her sari in the gaming-hall, and it unwound without end. Krishna had heard her; her honour could not be stripped.'

Name: Shakuni's Loaded Dice
Effect: On enter: Choose an opponent. They reveal their hand, and you may put one card from it at the bottom of their deck.
Anecdote: 'The dice are my bow and arrows, said Shakuni. The heart of the dice is my bowstring.'

Name: Shakuni, the Gambler
Effect: On enter: Each opponent must discard a card.
Anecdote: 'One more throw, said Shakuni gently. And Yudhishthira staked his kingdom, his brothers, himself — and lost them all.'

Name: Abhimanyu, the Young Warrior
Anecdote: 'He had learned in the womb how to enter the wheel-formation, but not how to leave it. Sixteen years old, he fought them all alone.'

Name: The Bhagavad Gita
Anecdote: 'My bow slips from my hand, said Arjuna. These are my teachers and my kin. — And between the two armies, Krishna began to speak.'

Name: Krishna, the Charioteer
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may move a friendly hero to another location.
Anecdote: 'I will not lift a weapon in this war, said Krishna. I will only hold the reins. It was worth more than all the armies.'

Name: Drona, the Master-at-Arms
Effect: On enter: Draw a hero from your deck.
Anecdote: 'Every archer on both sides of the field had learned the bow from him. Now his students drew against him.'

Name: Yudhishthira, the Just
Effect: On enter: Draw a card for each of your heroes in play.
Anecdote: 'His chariot rode a hand's breadth above the earth, for he had never told a lie. Then came the day of the elephant named Ashwatthama.'

Name: The Chakravyuha
Effect: While on top: Enemy beings cannot leave this location.
Anecdote: 'The wheel-formation turned, rank within rank. To enter it was hard; to leave it, no one living had been taught.'

Name: Duryodhana, the Envious
Anecdote: 'I know what is right, he said, but I have no taste for it. I know what is wrong, but I cannot leave it alone.'

Name: Karna, the Sun-Born
Anecdote: 'Indra came disguised as a beggar and asked for his golden armour. Karna cut it from his own body and gave it, knowing the trick.'

Name: Arjuna, the Focused
Effect: On enter: For each opponent, destroy an enemy being here with Power [3] or less. If 'Krishna, the Charioteer' is here, destroy their strongest being here instead.
Anecdote: 'What do you see? asked Drona. Only the eye of the bird, said Arjuna. Then shoot.'

Name: Bhima, the Strong
Effect: While on top: This card has +1 Power for each card the opponent controls at this location.
Anecdote: 'He swore in the gaming-hall to shatter the thigh of Duryodhana and drink the blood of Duhshasana. He kept both oaths.'

Name: Bhishma, the Grandsire
Effect: Indestructible. While on top: Once per turn, you may banish this being.
Anecdote: 'Death could not take him until he chose. On a bed of arrows he waited for the sun to turn north, teaching the kings as he died.'
